by-product/byproduct.ex: Heat is a natural by-product of this chemical reaction.

byroot.ex: The main root produced several horizontal byroots.

by-stander/bystander.ex: An innocent bystander was injured in the crossfire.

byway.def: 小徑、偏僻的小路、冷門的領域的人物、僻徑地 (徑)
byway.ex: They explored all the backstreets and byways of the city.

byword.def: 諺語、格言、典型、笑柄的人物 (典)
byword.ex: The name of the company became a byword for luxury.

cab.def: 計程車、馬車、(車)駕駛室、乘計程車、計程車的人物、計程車地 (車)
cab.col: taxi cab, cab driver, call a cab
cab.ex: I'll take a cab to the airport.

cabal.def: (政)陰謀集團、秘密夥黨、策劃陰謀、陰謀的人、陰謀地 (陰謀)
cabal.ex: A secret cabal of generals planned to overthrow the king.

cabaret.def: 卡巴萊(有歌舞表演的夜總會) (單數)
cabaret.ex: They spent the evening at a colorful cabaret show.

cabbage.def: 甘藍、捲心菜、(口)懶散的人、偷竊、懶散地 (菜)
cabbage.ex: Cabbage is a key ingredient in traditional coleslaw.

cabby.ex: The friendly cabby gave us some tips about the city.

cabin.def: 小木屋、船艙、機艙、住在木屋、木屋的人的人、木屋地 (木屋)
cabin.col: log cabin, cabin crew (機組人員), cabin pressure
cabin.ex: He built a small log cabin in the mountains.

cabinet.def: 內閣、內閣會議、櫥、櫃、內閣的、私人的、內閣的人、櫃子地 (閣)
cabinet.col: kitchen cabinet, filing cabinet, cabinet meeting, shadow cabinet (影子內閣)
cabinet.ex: The filing cabinet is full of old documents.

cabinet-maker.ex: A skilled cabinet-maker designed the mahogany desk.

cable.def: 電纜、纜繩、海底電報、發電報、電纜的人、電纜地 (纜)
cable.col: cable TV, steel cable, coaxial cable, connect via cable
cable.ex: The suspension bridge is held up by thick steel cables.

cablegram.ex: The explorer sent an urgent cablegram to the museum.

cabling.def: 裝電纜、電纜系統的人 (或物) (纜)
cabling.ex: The technicians are still working on the internal cabling.

cabman.ex: The London cabman knew every street in the city.

caboose.ex: The traditional red caboose was at the end of the train.

cabriolet.def: 開蓬小型馬車、開蓬汽車 (單數)
cabriolet.ex: They drove to the wedding in a sleek white cabriolet.

cacao.ex: The island's main export is dried cacao beans.

cache.def: 隱藏處、藏匿物、電腦快取、隱藏、快取的人、隱藏地 (藏)
cache.col: secret cache, memory cache, cache of weapons
cache.ex: The explorers found a secret cache of food and supplies.

cachet.def: 公文封印、優越標誌、名聲、藥囊的人物、名聲地 (名聲)
cachet.ex: The brand has a lot of cultural cachet among young people.

caching.ex: Data caching significantly improves the app's performance.

cackle.def: 咯咯叫、尖聲笑、閒談、咯咯叫的人、尖叫地 (笑)
cackle.ex: The old witch gave a wicked cackle.

cackling.def: 咯咯叫的、尖笑的的人 (或物) (笑)
cackling.ex: Her cackling laughter echoed through the empty hall.

cacophany/cacophony.def: 雜音、刺耳的聲音、且不和諧的人或物 (單數)
cacophany/cacophony.ex: The concert ended in a deafening cacophony of sound.

cacophonous.ex: We were greeted by a cacophonous roar of car horns.

cactus.ex: The desert cactus is adapted to survive in dry heat.

cad.ex: Don't listen to him; he's a complete cad.

cadaver.def: 屍體 (通常指供解剖用的) (單數)
cadaver.ex: The medical students were studying anatomy using a cadaver.
